raw.objective: “to determine the population mixed-effects model that describes compartmental PK and clearance of CBZ in adult epileptic patients using TDM” (translated from spanish)
raw.covariates: Included: BSA, daily dose in mg and use of phenytoin or primidone on CL
raw.observations: Ka, F, and Vd fixed on literature values. Strong bias in the final model. Dose recommendations are given in the discussion. A large amount of variability remains unexplained.
raw.equations: Ka=theta1 Vd/F=theta2*WT CL/F=(theta3*SC+theta4*DCBZ)*(1+theta5*PHT)*(1+theta6*PRIM)*(1+eta3) F=0.85 Cobs=Cipred+eps1 BSA=body surface area DCBZ=daily dose of carbamazepine in mg/day PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes =1, no=0) PRIM=comedication with primidone (yes =1, no=0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.65 theta2=1.76 theta3=0.614 theta4=0.0016 theta5=0.278 theta6=0.326 omega1=0.171 sigma1=1.74

extraction_target.title: [Population pharmacokinetics of carbamazepine in adults with epilepsy].

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

covariates: daily dose in mg and use of phenytoin or primidone on CL
extraction_target.residual_error: Ka=theta1 Vd/F=theta2*WT CL/F=(theta3*SC+theta4*DCBZ)*(1+theta5*PHT)*(1+theta6*PRIM)*(1+eta3) F=0.85 Cobs=Cipred+eps1 BSA=body surface area DCBZ=daily dose of carbamazepine in mg/day PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes =1, no=0) PRIM=comedication with primidone (yes =1, no=0)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to determine the population mixed-effects model that describes compartmental PK and clearance of CBZ in adult epileptic patients using TDM” (translated from spanish)

included: daily dose in mg and use of phenytoin or primidone on CL
covariates.raw: Included: BSA, daily dose in mg and use of phenytoin or primidone on CL

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
